free_text: 52Cr(d,p)53Cr, a test of distorted wave
stripping theory
No information in the article
Particles were detected by solid state
detectors capable of stopping the most energetic
protons observed.
DR
SD: In Subent 002 REACTION code was
corrected: SF5=PAR was added. In others - BIB updated.
Subents 5-7 were added.
Cross sections were determined by comparison with
Rutherford scattering at 3, 3.5 and 4 MeV
52Cr target enriched at 99.9 %
evaporated onto thin carbon backing and self-supporting
target
The 100.degr. spectrum was obtained using a
carbon-backed target, and the 50. degr. spectrum
was obtained using a self-supporting 52Cr target.
total error including:
Probable error due to counting statistics
was less than 4 % at all angles for the ground state
protons from the (d, p) reaction.
Probable error in cross sections due to
all sources (errors in normalization to Rutherford
scattering, errors in background subtraction, etc.,
in addition to statistics) is estimated to be less
than l0% for the ground state (d, p) protons.
Data taken from Fig. 3 NP,84,398,1966
SD: E-LVL was added and REACTION code was
corrected: SF5=PAR was added; DATA at Ed=4.69 and
6.02 MeV were multiplied by 10 according to the fig.3.
Cross sections were determined by comparison with
Rutherford scattering at 3, 3.5 and 4 MeV
52Cr target enriched at 99.9 %
evaporated onto thin carbon backing and self-supporting
target
The 100.degr. spectrum was obtained using a
carbon-backed target, and the 50. degr. spectrum
was obtained using a self-supporting 52Cr target.
Probable error due to counting statistics
was less than 2% at all angles for the elastically
scattered particles
Probable error in cross sections due to
all sources (errors in normalization to Rutherford
scattering, errors in background subtraction, etc.,
in addition to statistics) is estimated to be less
than 5% for the elastic scatterings.
Data taken from Table 1 of NP,84,398,1966

evaporated onto thin carbon backing.

than 5% for the elastic scatterings.
